campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that local companies have to pay. Finance department staff Shannon Dragman presented changes to the city's tax rules, and the council debated the proposal before making a final decision on the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates are being increased
Finance department presented the annual tax update
Council voted on changes to the Municipal Code
New tax rates will take effect in the next fiscal year

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could speak about a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, the council decided to move forward with the proposal and skipped the usual first reading process to speed things up. The ordinance will be voted on at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback on a new ordinance
First reading requirement was waived to expedite the adoption process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Details of the specific ordinance topic not specified in agenda

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council also authorized the town manager to sign contracts for construction work and professional services, though the full details of these projects were cut off in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed city finances and investment report for February 2026
Authorized construction contracts with multiple companies
Approved three-year professional services agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 29, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for May 5th and May 12th, 2026. The council reviewed what items to add to each meeting, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked whether any documents needed special notice waivers. The meeting also included a review of public records from mid-to-late April.
keyTopics: Planning the May 5, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Planning the May 12, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Scheduling specific times for certain agenda items
Review of public records from April 16-23, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and heard from residents during public comment. The main focus was deciding which neighborhood improvement projects would receive grant funding for the upcoming fiscal year, with council members discussing their recommendations and attending related neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes
Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27 fiscal year
Public comments and concerns from residents
Council attendance at neighborhood meetings

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on city boards and commissions. Residents were able to participate both in person and online to watch the interviews and provide public comments.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
In-person public comment opportunities
Online participation options for residents
Open positions being filled in city government

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 30, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 30, 2026, to approve meeting notes from the Economic Development Committee's January and March sessions. The council also opened the floor for residents to speak about local issues for up to 3 minutes each, though they noted the council is legally limited in making decisions on topics not formally listed on the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of January 29, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Approval of March 27, 2026 Economic Development Committee minutes
Public comment period for residents (3-minute limit per speaker)
State law limitations on decisions regarding non-agenda items

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss putting up large promotional signs around Levi's Stadium for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and to approve a new employment contract with the city's firefighters. One item was withdrawn from consideration before the meeting.
keyTopics: Large promotional signs approved for Levi's Stadium during World Cup 2026 events
New firefighter contract agreement (2026-2029) with Local 1171
Temporary signage rules for the stadium area during special events
Staff withdrew one agenda item from consideration

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 21, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held meetings to approve the city budget for 2026-27, update various fees and charges residents pay, and reaffirm commitment to a county homelessness prevention plan. The council also reviewed and approved meeting minutes and set the calendar for upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget and development fees approval
Updated user fees and service charges
County homelessness prevention initiative
Tax Equity and Fiscal Responsibility Act reissuance
City Council meeting schedule for upcoming months

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to consider a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine residential lots that would allow for nine new single-family homes and seven smaller accessory dwelling units. The council also handled routine business including scheduling meetings and electing leadership positions.
keyTopics: Proposal to subdivide 1.02-acre lot into 9 homes plus 7 accessory units on Old Trace Road
New private street planned to provide access to the residential development
Project exempted from lengthy environmental review under state law
Election of council chair and vice chair positions
